zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert IV Harington於1356年9月29日出生在英國藍卡爾阿爾迪加姆時，他的父親Sir John III Harington of Aldingham29歲，母親Joan Birmingham21歲。他於大約1376年與Elizabeth Greystoke結婚。他去世於1406年5月21日在他的家鄉享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert IV Harington于1356年9月29日出生在英国藍卡爾阿爾迪加姆时，他的父亲Sir John III Harington of Aldingham29岁，母亲Joan Birmingham21岁。他于大约1376年与Elizabeth Greystoke结婚。他去世于1406年5月21日在他的家乡享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: Harington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Harrington
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Harrington (Cumberland, Lincolnshire, Northamptonshire). The Cumberland placename derives from the Old English personal name Hæfer + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Lincolnshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Hearra + Old English connective -ing- + tūn. The Northamptonshire derives from an Old English personal name Hǣthhere + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Herendeen .
Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArrachtáin ‘descendant of Arrachtán’, a personal name from a diminutive of arrachtach ‘mighty, powerful’.
Irish: in Kerry, this name was adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hIongardail, later Ó hUrdáil, ‘descendant of Iongardal’, a personal name of uncertain origin.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert IV Harington was born on 29 September 1356, in Aldingham, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir John III Harington of Aldingham, was 29 and his mother, Joan Birmingham, was 21. He married Elizabeth Greystoke about 1376. He died on 21 May 1406, in his hometown, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert IV Harington est né le 29 septembre 1356, à Aldingham, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John III Harington of Aldingham, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Joan Birmingham, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Greystoke environ 1376. Il est décédé le 21 mai 1406, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert IV Harington am 29. September 1356 in Aldingham, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John III Harington of Aldingham 29 und seine Mutter Joan Birmingham 21. Er hat um 1376 Elizabeth Greystoke geheiratet. Er starb am 21. Mai 1406 im Alter von 49 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert IV Harington è nato il il 29 settembre 1356 nel Aldingham, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Sir John III Harington of Aldingham, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Joan Birmingham, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Greystoke circa 1376. Morì il 21 maggio 1406, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 49 anni.

ko.Knights made at the Coronation of Richar: 미얀마 မကွေးတိုင်းဒေသကြီး 매드
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert IV Harington(이)가 1356년 9월 29일 영국 랭카실르 알딩함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John III Harington of Aldingham(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Birmingham(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1376년 Elizabeth Greystoke(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1406년 5월 21일 그의 고향에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.29 September 1356–21 May 1406: 29 de setembro de 1356–21 de maio de 1406
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert IV Harington nasceu em 29 de setembro de 1356, em Aldingham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John III Harington of Aldingham, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Joan Birmingham, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Greystoke aproximadamente 1376. Ele faleceu em 21 de maio de 1406, em sua cidade natal, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert IV Harington родился 29 сентября 1356 г. в местности алдингхам, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John III Harington of Aldingham было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Birmingham, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Greystoke около 1376 г. Он умер 21 мая 1406 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 49 лет.

es.29 September 1356–21 May 1406: 29 de septiembre de 1356–21 de mayo de 1406
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert IV Harington nació el 29 de septiembre de 1356, en Aldingham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John III Harington of Aldingham, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Joan Birmingham, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Greystoke aproximadamente 1376. Murió el 21 de mayo de 1406, en su pueblo, a la edad de 49 años.
